reasoning: Prime Minister Milos Vucevic resigned in early 2025 amid anti-government protests, which initially raised the risk of snap elections. However, the ruling coalition successfully averted an automatic dissolution of parliament by forming a new government under political novice Djuro Macut in mid-April 2025. President Vucic’s explicit strategy has been to use this new cabinet to ride out the protests rather than go to the polls. Furthermore, on May 5, ruling party leader Vucevic flatly rejected student demands for a new vote. Given that the new administration was just seated in April, it is highly improbable that Vucic will dissolve it and announce new elections within its first 10 weeks (before July 1). Even if elections do occur later in H2 2025, an official announcement is very unlikely to come before the July 1 cutoff.
